name: Anti-King Chess
author: Peter Aronson (2002); platform port AGP
description: Anti-King Chess II (Peter Aronson, 2002): standard chess plus a second, inverted royal piece -- the Anti-King -- for each side (White on d6, Black on d3). An Anti-King is 'in check' only when it IS attacked: you must never end your turn with your own Anti-King un-attacked. The Anti-King moves like a king, may capture only friendly pieces, and can never be captured; kings do not attack anti-kings. Win by checkmating the enemy King OR anti-checkmating the enemy Anti-King. Standard pawns, castling, en passant and promotion to Q/R/B/N. White (player 0) moves first.
